es.def: Miembro en actividad, desde su iniciación hasta cumplir 21 años. La membresía es vitalicia: después pasa a ser Senior DeMolay.
El cuerpo de adultos certificados que supervisa el Chapter: mínimo 5 advisors, con al menos 3 Master Masons en good standing. Nombrado por el Executive Officer.
Oficial designado a cargo de la caridad del Chapter; responsable del Day of Comfort.
El boletín oficial de DeMolay International: información, inspiración y unificación. Cada Chapter debería tener un reportero.
El reporte anual del Chapter, preparado en enero por el Scribe y el Chairman vía eScribe. Es el único método de información del Grand Secretary.
Los cuerpos anexos a DeMolay: Squires (9-11 años), Order of Knighthood (17-21), Court of Chevaliers y Preceptories of the Legion of Honor.
Reconocimiento que se gana cumpliendo requisitos y puede solicitarse (a diferencia de un Honor). Ejemplos: Merit Bars, Blue Honor Key.
El voto secreto con bolas blancas y cubos negros: 0-1 black cube elige, 2 deja la solicitud en suspenso, 3 o más la rechazan.
Miembro mayor asignado por el Master Councilor para acompañar a cada nuevo miembro durante su orientación.
Premio por reclutamiento: 10 Membership Applications como first-line signer; una estrella por cada 10 adicionales.
El reglamento del Chapter, uniforme según los Bylaws of DeMolay International y aprobado por el Executive Officer. El original lo guarda el Scribe.
Ceremonia pública con siete velas que representan las siete virtudes cardinales. Cero desviación permitida del texto oficial.
Oficial designado que dirige las oraciones del Chapter; responsable del Devotional Day.
La unidad local de DeMolay: jóvenes de 12 a 21 dirigidos por sus propios oficiales y asesorados por un Advisory Council.
El adulto mentor principal del Chapter, designado por el Executive Officer. Elige la Word of the Day y firma las obligation cards.
La carta constitutiva permanente de un Chapter. Se obtiene tras operar con éxito bajo Letters Temporary (mínimo 15 miembros).
El honor más alto para un DeMolay activo: 17+ años, 2 de membresía, voto unánime del ISC. Nunca se solicita. Observancia: 8 de noviembre.
La convención anual de una Jurisdiction: competencias de ritual, deportes, elecciones y hermandad.
Cuerpo integrado solo por Chevaliers que confiere el Degree of Chevalier a los nombrados por el Supreme Council.
El máximo honor para Advisors: al menos 3 años de servicio meritorio conspicuo, votado por el Supreme Council.
La corona de la juventud: símbolo central del emblema DeMolay y elemento ritual requerido en la sala del Chapter.
Obligatory Day de la caridad: entre el 15 de noviembre y el 31 de diciembre, a cargo del Almoner.
El segundo grado de DeMolay: el drama de la lealtad de Jacques DeMolay. Se confiere de memoria, idealmente con escenario.
La organización mundial, con sede en el Service & Leadership Center de Kansas City, gobernada por el International Supreme Council.
La semana de celebración que incluye el 18 de marzo, con proclamación oficial y, idealmente, un evento por día.
Obligatory Day religioso en la semana del 18 de marzo (aniversario del martirio de Jacques DeMolay), a cargo del Chaplain.
El "DeMolay of the Year": evalúa escuela, hogar, culto, comunidad, país y DeMolay. Ganadores jurisdiccionales y uno internacional.
Obligatory Day de la educación, usualmente en septiembre, a cargo de los Junior y Senior Stewards.
El portal online de DeMolay International para reportar miembros, premios, certificados y el Annual Report. Sin reporte, el miembro no está registrado.
El representante oficial de DeMolay International en una Jurisdiction. Su aprobación es necesaria para casi todo: Chapters, advisors, bylaws, fundraising externo.
El primer firmante que recomienda una Membership Application. Es lo que cuenta para la Blue Honor Key y el FMA.
Ceremonia pública de ~15 minutos sobre el amor a la madre: flores rojas y blancas (blanca por madre fallecida). De memoria; no es parte del degree.
Premio por ser first-line signer en 5 Membership Applications. Sin cargo.
"Dad" Land (1890-1959), fundador de DeMolay y su Secretary-General desde 1919 hasta su muerte, el 8 de noviembre de 1959.
La máxima autoridad de DeMolay International, electa anualmente por el International Supreme Council.
Reconocimiento que el Chapter vota para agradecer a cualquier persona. No se solicita y no tiene límite.
Reconocimiento que NO se solicita: se otorga sin conocimiento del receptor por voto unánime del ISC (Chevalier, Legion of Honor).
El primer grado de DeMolay: enseña las siete virtudes. Se confiere con sinceridad, sin dramatización, y siempre de memoria.
El cuerpo de gobierno de DeMolay International, integrado por Masons adultos. Nació como Grand Council en 1921; nombre actual desde 1954.
Último Grand Master de los Knights Templars (1244-1314), quemado en la hoguera el 18 de marzo de 1314 por negarse a traicionar a sus compañeros.
El territorio administrativo de DeMolay (estado, provincia o país) a cargo de un Executive Officer.
Pin que se obtiene al completar los cinco cursos del Leaders Correspondence Course.
Curso online de liderazgo en BeADeMolay.org/lcc: 5 lecciones, gratis, 24/7. Cada lección da una Merit Bar.
El gran honor para Senior DeMolays mayores de 25: voto unánime del ISC bajo confidencialidad estricta. Observancia: 18 de marzo.
La autorización provisoria de un Chapter nuevo, emitida por orden del Grand Master, hasta alcanzar 15 miembros y obtener el Charter.
La membresía vitalicia prepaga incluida en el initiation fee. Cada plan de Life Membership requiere aprobación del Executive Officer.
El paso a Senior DeMolay al cumplir 21, y la ceremonia que lo celebra. El certificado se genera en eScribe.
La unidad local de los Squires of the Round Table, patrocinada por un Advisory Council o una Jurisdiction.
Oficial designado que conduce los movimientos en la sala y asiste en la tabulación de votos; co-responsable del My Government Day.
El presidente del Chapter, electo por los miembros. Preside desde el East, nombra los comités y arma el programa del término.
Honor por arriesgar voluntariamente la propia vida. Creada en 1925; menos de 50 otorgadas desde entonces.
La solicitud formal de ingreso, con consentimiento de padres/tutores. Se presenta en stated meeting al menos 1 semana antes del ballot.
Los premios más comunes: barras por categorías de trabajo (White → Red → Blue → Purple → Gold). La decisión final es del Advisory Council.
El manual de ceremonias públicas del Chapter, descargable del sitio de DeMolay.
El Chapter original de Kansas City, fundado en 1919; recibió el Charter N.º 1.
Obligatory Day cívico de julio, a cargo del Orator y el Marshal.
Ceremonia breve obligatoria en TODA reunión DeMolay, pública o privada: a las nueve, el recuerdo de las madres y los padres.
El compromiso ritual de cada grado. Memorizar ambas Obligations es requisito de proficiency para votar y ocupar cargos.
La tarjeta firmada por el Chapter Advisor que acredita tus Obligations aprobadas. Sin ella no votás ni ocupás cargo.
Las siete observancias anuales obligatorias: Devotional, Patriots', Parents', My Government, Educational, Frank S. Land Memorial y Day of Comfort.
Cuerpo anexo para DeMolays activos de 17 a 21 años, organizado en Priories. Más de la mitad de su programa debe ser servicio.
El programa de 3 sesiones que acompaña al nuevo miembro desde su application hasta recitar la DeMolay Degree Obligation.
Obligatory Day de los padres, entre el 1 de mayo y el 21 de junio, a cargo de los siete Preceptors.
El certificado oficial de membresía, emitido por el Service & Leadership Center cuando ambos Degrees están reportados y pagos.
Obligatory Day patriótico de febrero, a cargo del Standard Bearer.
La joya al Past Master Councilor sobresaliente: el único regalo que el Chapter puede pagar. Exige ambos Degrees conferidos de memoria.
El Past Master Councilor's Meritorious Service Award: medalla de oro de DI por un término sobresaliente como MC, con requisitos estrictos.
Cada uno de los siete oficiales que representan las virtudes cardinales con sus velas. Responsables del Parents' Day.
La unidad local de la Order of Knighthood, dirigida por el Illustrious Knight Commander.
El examen de las Obligations de ambos grados, tomado por el Senior Deacon en Chapter abierto y decidido por voto del Chapter.
La máxima distinción de auto-logro: autoevaluación en 4 campos (mental, físico, socio-económico, espiritual). Desde 1935.
El texto ceremonial de DeMolay, escrito por Frank A. Marshall en 1919. Todo el trabajo de grado se da de memoria, sin desviación.
El libro del ritual reservado del Chapter. Es propiedad de DeMolay International y debe devolverse a requerimiento.
El secretario del Chapter, nombrado por el Advisory Council: minutas, eScribe, reportes y el Annual Report. El tercer cargo más importante.
Todo DeMolay que cumplió 21 años. La membresía es de por vida: puede volver como Advisor o sumarse a la Alumni Association.
El custodio de la puerta del Chapter: recibe la Word of the Day y no admite a nadie durante la apertura.
La sede central de DeMolay International en Kansas City (Henry E. "Hank" Stickney Center): registros, servicio, promoción y unificación.
Los siete valores centrales: love of parents, reverence, courtesy, comradeship, fidelity, cleanness y patriotism.
La organización (normalmente masónica) que patrocina al Chapter: le provee lugar de reunión y sostiene su Advisory Council.
El programa para chicos de 9 a 11 años, organizado en Manors. Fundado en 1995 y adoptado por DeMolay International en 2015.
El oficial que porta la bandera; responsable del Patriots' Day.
La reunión regular de negocios del Chapter, fijada en sus Bylaws.
La joven elegida por el Chapter como su representante y compañera de honor, tradicionalmente coronada en un baile.
La regla de oro de Youth Protection: mínimo 2 registered Advisors presentes en toda actividad, sin excepción.
Premio por reclutar 3 nuevos Advisors registrados; estrellas por cada 3 adicionales.
La contraseña de ingreso a la reunión, elegida por el Chapter Advisor y comunicada al Sentinel.
El programa de protección juvenil de DeMolay: Two-Deep Leadership, Rule of Four, ratios de supervisión, prohibición de hazing y reporte obligatorio.
Premio por ayudar a fundar un Chapter: hasta 3 por Chapter nuevo, otorgadas dentro del año de la institución.

en.def: A member in activity, from initiation until age 21. Membership is for life: afterwards he becomes a Senior DeMolay.
The certified adult body overseeing the Chapter: minimum 5 advisors, with at least 3 Master Masons in good standing. Appointed by the Executive Officer.
Appointed officer in charge of the Chapter's charity; responsible for the Day of Comfort.
DeMolay International's official newsletter: information, inspiration and unification. Every Chapter should have a reporter.
The Chapter's yearly report, prepared in January by the Scribe and the Chairman via eScribe. The Grand Secretary's only source of information.
DeMolay's allied bodies: Squires (ages 9-11), Order of Knighthood (17-21), Court of Chevaliers and Preceptories of the Legion of Honor.
Recognition earned by meeting requirements and which may be applied for (unlike an Honor). Examples: Merit Bars, Blue Honor Key.
The secret vote with white balls and black cubes: 0-1 black cube elects, 2 makes the application lie over, 3 or more reject it.
An older member assigned by the Master Councilor to guide each new member through orientation.
Recruiting award: 10 Membership Applications as first-line signer; a star for every additional 10.
The Chapter's rules, uniform per the Bylaws of DeMolay International and approved by the Executive Officer. The Scribe keeps the original.
Public ceremony with seven candles representing the seven cardinal virtues. Zero deviation from the official text allowed.
Appointed officer who leads the Chapter's prayers; responsible for Devotional Day.
DeMolay's local unit: young men aged 12 to 21 led by their own officers and advised by an Advisory Council.
The Chapter's lead adult mentor, appointed by the Executive Officer. Chooses the Word of the Day and signs obligation cards.
A Chapter's permanent constitutive document, earned after operating successfully under Letters Temporary (at least 15 members).
The highest honor for an active DeMolay: age 17+, 2 years of membership, unanimous ISC vote. Never applied for. Observance: November 8.
A Jurisdiction's annual convention: ritual competitions, sports, elections and brotherhood.
A body composed only of Chevaliers that confers the Degree of Chevalier on those named by the Supreme Council.
The highest honor for Advisors: at least 3 years of conspicuous meritorious service, voted by the Supreme Council.
The crown of youth: the central symbol of the DeMolay emblem and a required ritual item in the Chapter room.
The Obligatory Day of charity: between November 15 and December 31, led by the Almoner.
DeMolay's second degree: the drama of Jacques DeMolay's loyalty. Conferred from memory, ideally on a stage.
The worldwide organization, headquartered at the Service & Leadership Center in Kansas City, governed by the International Supreme Council.
The celebration week including March 18, with an official proclamation and ideally one event per day.
The religious Obligatory Day in the week of March 18 (anniversary of Jacques DeMolay's martyrdom), led by the Chaplain.
The "DeMolay of the Year": evaluates school, home, worship, community, country and DeMolay. Jurisdictional winners and one international.
The Obligatory Day of education, usually in September, led by the Junior and Senior Stewards.
DeMolay International's online portal for reporting members, awards, certificates and the Annual Report. Unreported members aren't registered.
DeMolay International's official representative in a Jurisdiction. His approval is needed for almost everything: Chapters, advisors, bylaws, outside fundraising.
The first recommender signing a Membership Application. What counts toward the Blue Honor Key and the FMA.
A ~15-minute public ceremony about a mother's love: red and white flowers (white for a deceased mother). From memory; not part of the degree.
Award for being first-line signer on 5 Membership Applications. No charge.
"Dad" Land (1890-1959), DeMolay's Founder and Secretary-General from 1919 until his death on November 8, 1959.
DeMolay International's highest authority, elected annually by the International Supreme Council.
Recognition the Chapter votes to thank anyone. Cannot be applied for and has no limit.
Recognition that is NEVER applied for: conferred without the recipient's knowledge by unanimous ISC vote (Chevalier, Legion of Honor).
DeMolay's first degree: teaches the seven virtues. Conferred with sincerity, no dramatics, always from memory.
DeMolay International's governing body of adult Masons. Born as the Grand Council in 1921; current name since 1954.
Last Grand Master of the Knights Templar (1244-1314), burned at the stake on March 18, 1314, for refusing to betray his companions.
DeMolay's administrative territory (state, province or country) under an Executive Officer.
The pin earned by completing all five Leaders Correspondence Course lessons.
The online leadership course at BeADeMolay.org/lcc: 5 lessons, free, 24/7. Each lesson earns a Merit Bar.
The great honor for Senior DeMolays over 25: unanimous ISC vote under strict confidentiality. Observance: March 18.
A new Chapter's provisional authority, issued by order of the Grand Master, until it reaches 15 members and earns its Charter.
The prepaid lifetime membership included in the initiation fee. Each Life Membership plan requires the Executive Officer's approval.
Becoming a Senior DeMolay at 21, and the ceremony celebrating it. The certificate is created in eScribe.
The local unit of the Squires of the Round Table, sponsored by an Advisory Council or a Jurisdiction.
Appointed officer who conducts floor movements and helps tabulate votes; co-responsible for My Government Day.
The Chapter's president, elected by the members. Presides from the East, appoints committees and builds the term program.
Honor for voluntarily risking one's own life. Created in 1925; fewer than 50 awarded since.
The formal joining request, with parent/guardian consent. Presented at a stated meeting at least 1 week before the ballot.
The most common awards: bars per work category (White → Red → Blue → Purple → Gold). The Advisory Council has the final say.
The Chapter's public-ceremony manual, downloadable from the DeMolay website.
The original Kansas City Chapter, founded in 1919; holder of Charter No. 1.
July's civic Obligatory Day, led by the Orator and the Marshal.
A brief ceremony required at EVERY DeMolay meeting, public or private: at nine o'clock, the remembrance of mothers and fathers.
Each degree's ritual commitment. Memorizing both Obligations is the proficiency requirement for voting and holding office.
The card signed by the Chapter Advisor proving your approved Obligations. Without it you cannot vote or hold office.
The seven mandatory yearly observances: Devotional, Patriots', Parents', My Government, Educational, Frank S. Land Memorial and Day of Comfort.
The appendant body for active DeMolays aged 17 to 21, organized in Priories. Over half its program must be service.
The 3-session program guiding a new member from his application until he recites the DeMolay Degree Obligation.
The parents' Obligatory Day, between May 1 and June 21, led by the seven Preceptors.
The official membership certificate, issued by the Service & Leadership Center once both Degrees are reported and paid.
February's patriotic Obligatory Day, led by the Standard Bearer.
The jewel for an outstanding Past Master Councilor: the only gift a Chapter may pay for. Requires both Degrees conferred from memory.
The Past Master Councilor's Meritorious Service Award: DI's gold medal for an outstanding term as MC, with strict requirements.
Each of the seven officers representing the cardinal virtues with their candles. Responsible for Parents' Day.
The local unit of the Order of Knighthood, led by the Illustrious Knight Commander.
The examination on both degrees' Obligations, conducted by the Senior Deacon in open Chapter and decided by Chapter vote.
The highest self-achievement distinction: a self-evaluation across 4 fields (mental, physical, socio-economic, spiritual). Since 1935.
DeMolay's ceremonial text, written by Frank A. Marshall in 1919. All degree work is delivered from memory, with no deviation.
The Chapter's reserved ritual book. It is DeMolay International's property and must be returned on demand.
The Chapter's secretary, appointed by the Advisory Council: minutes, eScribe, reports and the Annual Report. The third most important office.
Any DeMolay who has turned 21. Membership is for life: he may return as an Advisor or join the Alumni Association.
The keeper of the Chapter door: receives the Word of the Day and admits no one during the opening.
DeMolay International's headquarters in Kansas City (the Henry E. "Hank" Stickney Center): records, service, promotion and unification.
The seven core values: love of parents, reverence, courtesy, comradeship, fidelity, cleanness and patriotism.
The organization (usually Masonic) sponsoring the Chapter: it provides a meeting place and sustains its Advisory Council.
The program for boys aged 9 to 11, organized in Manors. Established in 1995 and adopted by DeMolay International in 2015.
The officer who carries the flag; responsible for Patriots' Day.
The Chapter's regular business meeting, fixed in its Bylaws.
The young woman chosen as the Chapter's representative and companion of honor, traditionally crowned at a ball.
Youth Protection's golden rule: at least 2 registered Advisors present at every activity, no exceptions.
Award for recruiting 3 new registered Advisors; stars for every additional 3.
The meeting password, chosen by the Chapter Advisor and given to the Sentinel.
DeMolay's youth protection program: Two-Deep Leadership, Rule of Four, supervision ratios, hazing prohibition and mandatory reporting.
Award for helping found a Chapter: up to 3 per new Chapter, granted within a year of institution.
